commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=e7f40ff7e546bdc0a0268dbb4166b279ba8ebe98)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a core `dummy/dummy` deterministic gateway model, exposes it in the default-model picker, accepts it as `agents.defaults.model.primary`, and returns a fixed reply before entering the agent harness.

PR surface: Source +31, Tests +88. Total +119 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: setup applies model selection and then calls `warnIfModelConfigLooksOff`, whose current auth check warns whenever the selected provider lacks credentials. I did not run the setup flow because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A maintainer should decide the core-versus-plugin contract first; if core is accepted, the auth/setup diagnostic repair is focused and should happen before merge.

target.title: feat(gateway): add deterministic dummy model (AI-assisted)
target.body:
## Summary

- Add `dummy/dummy` as an explicit “No AI - deterministic gateway only” model in the interactive default-model picker and support the same value when written directly to `openclaw.json`.
- Return a deterministic help response after command and plugin interception, but before entering the agent harness or calling a model provider.
- This prevents customers who intentionally use OpenClaw only for channels, webhooks, cron, and deterministic plugins from seeing provider errors when they type ordinary text into chat.
- Treat this as a small gateway-lite precursor toward a future distribution or mode that can run entirely without the AI harness.
- Intentionally out of scope: disabling model-oriented slash commands such as `/models`. A future gateway-only profile should consider hiding or disabling commands that do not apply, but this PR does not bundle that policy change.
- This could alternatively be packaged as a plugin. Because the interception point sits deep in the core reply pipeline between deterministic command/plugin handling and the agent harness, an official implementation maintained by the OpenClaw developers would provide a cleaner and more stable contract.

## Linked context

Related #86881

Related discussion comment: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86881#issuecomment-4558360604

This PR implements the later `dummy/dummy` refinement discussed on that issue without claiming to complete the broader harness-free architecture.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: configure a deterministic gateway model without provider credentials and without provider/model errors for ordinary chat text.
- Real environment tested: Linux host, fresh shallow checkout of OpenClaw `main`, Node.js 24.15.0.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<temporary-dir> node openclaw.mjs config set agents.defaults.model.primary dummy/dummy`, followed by `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<temporary-dir> node openclaw.mjs config get agents.defaults.model.primary`.
- Evidence after fix: CLI output was `Updated agents.defaults.model.primary. Restart the gateway to apply.` followed by `dummy/dummy`.
- Observed result after fix: the direct configuration path accepts and persists `dummy/dummy`; focused runtime tests verify ordinary text returns the deterministic response while command/plugin interception remains ahead of the harness guard.
- What was not tested: live Telegram or another external channel, and a human-driven interactive TTY recording of the picker.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: interactive selection and harness bypass are covered by focused tests; no external channel credentials were used for this upstream checkout.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/commands/model-picker.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.before-agent-reply.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.defaults.test.ts` (70 tests passed)
- `oxlint` on all changed TypeScript files
- `oxfmt` on all changed TypeScript files
- Full `pnpm build` using official Node.js 24.15.0 (passed)

Regression coverage verifies:

- `dummy/dummy` is selectable even when no provider catalog is available.
- direct `openclaw.json` configuration resolves to provider `dummy` and model `dummy`.
- an already configured deterministic model is not duplicated in the picker.
- plugin hooks can still handle a message before the deterministic fallback.
- ordinary text in deterministic mode does not enter the agent harness.

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior changed: Yes, only when the effective model is exactly `dummy/dummy`.
- Config, environment, or migration behavior changed: Yes, `agents.defaults.model.primary` now has a supported deterministic value; no migration is required.
- Security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior changed: No.
- Highest-risk area: reply pipeline ordering.
- Mitigation: the guard runs after native/inline command handling and `before_agent_reply` hooks, but immediately before media staging and `runPreparedReply`; focused tests cover that ordering.

## Current review state

- Next action: maintainer review and CI.
- Waiting on: upstream judgment on whether this core gateway-lite precursor is preferable to an official plugin contract.
- AI-assisted: Yes. The implementation was produced with Codex and reviewed through targeted tests, lint, formatting, and a full build.
